name: Fettuccine with Shiitake Sauce and Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Beef stock or canned unsalted beef broth
3 each Boneless chicken breast, cut in strips
2/3 cup Freshly grated parmesan cheese (about 2 ounces)
4 oz Fresh shiitake mushrooms or Button mushrooms, sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place dried mushrooms in a medium bowl. Add hot water and let soak until softened, about 30 minutes.
2. Remove mushrooms from water, squeezing the excess water back into the bowl. Strain and reserve liquid.
3. Slice mushrooms, discarding stems.
4. Heat oil in a heavy large skillet over medium heat. Add bacon and chicken; cook until fat renders, stirring frequently, about 3 minutes.
5. Add dried and fresh mushrooms and rosemary; sauté 3 minutes.
6. Pour off half of the fat.
7. Add shallots to bacon in skillet; sauté 1 minute.
8. Add dried and fresh mushrooms and rosemary; sauté 3 minutes.
9. Add stock and mushroom-soaking liquid. Boil until reduced to a glaze, about 10 minutes.
10. Add half-and-half; simmer until beginning to thicken, about 5 minutes.
11. Remove from heat (can be prepared up to 3 days ahead, cover and refrigerate).
12. Add pasta to a large pot of boiling salted water; cook until tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally.
13. Meanwhile, rewarm mushroom sauce over medium-low heat.
14. Drain pasta. Add to sauce and stir until coated.
15. Mix in 2/3 cup Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
16. Transfer to plates. Sprinkle with parsley. Serve immediately with garlic toasts; pass additional Parmesan separately (see recipe for garlic toasts).
